Gloomhaven isn't known for its well-mannered folk and good Samaritans, but there's a lot to gain from its city streets... You can choose to <color=#EACF8C>partake in City Encounters</color> by entering Gloomhaven on map or or press {UI_SUBMIT} to select the entry in the Quest Log. (It's encouraged that you do this whenever you're in the city, as they often give good rewards).

The <color=#EACF8C>first Quest</color> Location is marked just north of Gloomhaven, you’ll see it flagged with a quest icon. As you progress more quest locations will appear across the map. To keep track of them you can use the Quest Log on the right hand side of the screen.
Select the Black Barrow’s location or use {UI_SUBMIT} to select the entry in the Quest Log.

On the left you’ll find the <color=#EACF8C>Party Panel</color>. Here you’ll see the basic information of the Mercenaries in your Party (at least, you will once you add your first Mercenary).
<b>Use {UI_PREVIOUS_TAB} to access the <color=#EACF8C>Party Panel</color>.<b>
Use {UI_SUBMIT} to <color=#EACF8C>select the “New Mercenary” button</color> and create a character

You’ll find all your Mercenaries and add them to the Party through the Roster Panel. Right now it’s looking as barren as the Copperneck Mountains, use {UI_SUBMIT} to <color=#EACF8C>select the “New Mercenary” button</color> and create your first character!

Next you'll choose one of the <color=#EACF8C>starting Character Classes</color>.
Each one has unique abilities and game style. As you progress you'll Level Up, earning new Ability Cards and unlock more Classes. But remember, looks aren’t everything; Merc’s Ability Cards are what define their actions in battle. Use {CREATOR_SHOW_ABILITY_CARDS} to <color=#EACF8C>select the “Starting Ability Cards”</color> button to make sure this is the right Mercenary for your party.
Once you are sure of your decision, use {UI_SUBMIT} to <color=#EACF8C>select the Mercenary</color> and move forward.
Use {UI_PREVIOUS_TAB} to access the <color=#EACF8C>Party Panel</color> and select the second character Slot

Use {UI_PREVIOUS_TAB} to select the <color=#EACF8C>Second Character Slot</color> in the Party Panel and create a New Character.
Know that you can't have more than one Merc of the same character class in the party at the same time, so be sure to create a different one!
Use {PREVIOUS_SHIELD_TAB} or {NEXT_SHIELD_TAB} to inspect the Town Options and access <color=#EACF8C>the Merchant's Shop</color>
This application uses an auto-save feature. Please do not close your application when you see the auto-save icon.

You’ve unlocked a City Quest!
You can use {PREVIOUS_SHIELD_TAB} or {NEXT_SHIELD_TAB} to inspect the Town Options and use {UI_SUBMIT} to <color=#EACF8C>select the City Map</color>, or directly selecting Gloomhaven on the World Map or by selecting the new Quest in the Log.

Now might be a good time to go through the Mercs <color=#EACF8C>Ability Cards and Inventory</color> to make sure they are battle ready. In the Quest Description you can see the enemies you’ll face. Inspect their stats by pushing {MOVE_CAMERA_RIGHT}.
Try to take damage to your hit points over burning cards – it's often easier to regain health than recover burned cards.
Watch out for traps! You can see them glinting occasionally to help you spot them.
You can use Push and Pull abilities to force enemies onto traps.
Look for synergies not only between your cards, but also the cards of your other party members.
Move abilities on the top half of a card and Attack abilities on the bottom half of a card are uncommon, but can be especially useful.
You can adjust the difficulty to your liking if you find yourself struggling or craving a challenge.
Consider whether you want to act early or late in a round – letting the enemy move towards you first can be advantageous.
To consume elements to augment an ability, click the toggles that appear before confirming targets for the ability.
Be prepared for more enemies when you open a door to another room!
Choose your party before travelling – once you are on your way, you can no longer switch out party members.
For those really tough missions in Guildmaster mode, consider seeking a blessing from the Temple beforehand.
Guildmaster's Job quests have a chance of spawning a chest – be sure to loot it before finishing the scenario objectives!
Be careful when equipping new Items – they'll be bound to that mercenary. You'll have to pay to re-equip them elsewhere.
You can unlock more enhancement types by completing achievements, and more slots per mercenary by exploring the map.
Guildmaster's Travel and Job quests scale as you play through the game. Story and Relic quests tend to be tougher!
Not all scenarios require all enemies to be killed to be completed – be sure to check your objectives.
Allies can be helpful in scenarios, but are often a little reckless – try to divert attacks from them to keep them alive!
If you are unsure if you will get to act early or late, consider picking a pair of cards that can be useful regardless of when you get to act.
Observe the enemy moves and learn their decks and abilities. Some creatures are consistently fast and others slow, which is knowledge you can use to your advantage.
Each card half you select for a player character's turn is an action, and is comprised of one or more abilities.
An action with a specified type means that at least one of the abilities within are of that type. For example, a "Move action" contains at least one Move ability.
Whenever you Push/Pull X, you must always Push/Pull the target the full amount (unless obstacles/characters prevent further movement), or skip the effect altogether.
Beware that if a Miss (x0) attack modifier is drawn along with a rolling modifier while you have Advantage, the miss will still apply!
Enemies add +1 Attack when attacking a Poisoned <sprite name=Poison> character. A Heal ability will cure Poison but have no other effect.
Wound <sprite name=Wound> causes the character to suffer one damage at the start of each of its turns. A Heal ability will cure the Wound.
Immobilize <sprite name=Immobilize> prevents a character from performing any Move abilities until the end of their next turn.
Disarm <sprite name=Disarm> prevents a character from performing any Attack abilities until the end of their next turn.
Stun <sprite name=Stun> prevents a character from performing any actions or using items until the end of their next turn.
Stunned <sprite name=Stun> characters must still select cards for their turn, but they are simply discarded. Long Rests are performed as normal.
Muddle <sprite name=Muddle> causes a character to gain Disadvantage on all attacks until the end of their next turn.
Curse <sprite name=Curse> adds a CURSE (x0) card to the target's attack modifier deck. When drawn, the attack deals no damage, and the CURSE is removed from the deck.
Invisible <sprite name=Invisible> characters cannot be focused nor targeted by enemies until the end of their next turn. Monsters treat invisible characters as if they were obstacles.
Invisible <sprite name=Invisible> characters can still be damaged by non-targeted damage. Invisibility has no effect on interactions with allies.
Strengthen <sprite name=Strengthen> grants a character Advantage on all their attacks until the end of their next turn.
Bless <sprite name=Bless> adds a BLESS (x2) card to the target's attack modifier deck. When drawn, the attack deals double damage, and the BLESS is removed from the deck.
Monsters share an attack modifier deck, so if a monster receives a Curse or Bless, it could be another monster that draws that effect!
Note that abilities that remove negative conditions, such as Cure potions, can not remove Curses from the attack modifier deck.

There is a 10 Curse limit shared between mercenary attack modifier decks, and another separate limit of 10 for the monster attack modifier deck. Trying to add an 11th Curse to any deck will have no effect!
There is a shared 10 Bless limit among all attack modifier decks. Trying to add a Bless to any attack modifier deck while 10 Blesses are already in play will have no effect!
Down to only a pair of discarded cards? Short Resting would lead to exhaustion; consider Long Resting and be a meat shield!
If a non-aggressive character is attempting to move to a location but the route is blocked, then that character will not move.
It's up to you whether to have 2, 3 or 4 mercenaries in your party – the difficulty of a scenario will scale based on how many you bring along.
Choices made during Road and City Events can affect the world, potentially causing new quests and events to become available.
Unlike normal chests, you do not get to keep quest items looted from Ornate Chests if you fail the scenario.
Summons can only be summoned onto empty hexes - traps, difficult terrain and even gold will block summons!
A private save with this name already exists. Do you want to overwrite the existing private save with the current state of game?

By the way, if you're struggling to make out objects and enemies in the undergrowth, you can press {HIGHLIGHT} to highlight them. You can also see the turn order of monsters of the same class this way. They will all get the same initiative from the card drawn for their class, but the actual order per monster on that class' turn is determined by their ID numbers. Lower numbers act first!
I’ll leave you to finish off the rest of these bad guys. Did you know you can rotate the shape of an Area Effect by pressing {UI_RETRY}?
In this tutorial, the Brute has just two cards at his disposal, as you can see highlighted on the left: Grab and Go, Trample.

In Gloomhaven, you control a party of mercenaries, each equipped with a deck of Ability Cards. These cards are how your mercenaries will explore dungeons, fight enemies and loot treasure. At the beginning of each round, the first thing to do is to select two cards for each mercenary to use on their turn.
Press the Confirm Targets button to attack!
Press the Confirm Movement button to continue
Press the Open Door button to confirm the move
Whilst hovered, you can see the Bandit's intentions this round: he plans to Move and Attack!
Whilst focused, you can see the Bandit's intentions this round: he plans to Move and Attack!

This room contains your first enemy, a Bandit Guard! Like player characters, monsters also select actions for their turn - let's find out what this Bandit is planning to do this round by selecting it in top panel.

This room contains your first enemy, a Bandit Guard! Like player characters, monsters also select actions for their turn - let's find out what this Bandit is planning to do this round by focusing over his portrait in the top panel.
Now that you've finished using Grab and Go for the turn, it is discarded and no longer usable until you return it to hand via resting – more on that later. For now, time to Attack!
Select the Bandit's portrait in the Initiative Tracker at the top of the screen
Hover over the Bandit's portrait in the Initiative Tracker at the top of the screen

See how the '?' next to the Brute's portrait at the top of the screen has changed into a number? This shows his initiative. The number next to the first card you select determines the character's initiative for the round. The lower the number, the earlier in the round the character will act.

Notice how this Bandit has a lower initiative than the Brute, and would have acted before the Brute this round had he been visible. As it is currently the Brute’s turn however, you'll get to finish his turn before the Bandit can act.
Let's get the Brute close enough to use the Attack on Trample's top half as the second action for the turn!

There's nothing for the Brute to do in this room, so you'll want to proceed through the door to see what lies beyond with a Move ability. Grab and Go's bottom half lets the Brute move four hexes, which is enough to get through the door. Let's use that!
Select the Bandit Guard as the target for the attack
...or the bottom half of Trample and the top half of Grab and Go.
Select the bottom half of Grab and Go
Select the highlighted hex next to the Bandit
For example, here you can choose either the top half of Trample and the bottom half of Grab and Go...
Select the top half of Trample

Now it is the Brute's turn, time to choose some actions. Each half of a card represents an action, comprised of one or more abilities. You get to use two actions per turn, and you can pick any of the four card halves on the left to use as your first action – but note that you must then use the opposite side of the other card for your second.

Before we get to the action, you’ll want to acquaint yourself with how to adjust your view of the battlefield, so let’s go over the camera controls. L-stick - Pan camera R-stick left/right - Rotate camera left/right Right-stick top/down - Zoom camera in/out You can also rotate the camera by dragging your mouse with the middle mouse button held. Try them out now and press Continue when you're ready to move on.

Before we get to the action, you’ll want to acquaint yourself with how to adjust your view of the battlefield, so let’s go over the camera controls. L-stick - Pan camera R-stick left/right - Rotate camera left/right Right-stick top/down - Zoom camera in/out You can also rotate the camera by dragging your mouse with the middle mouse button held. Try them out now and press Continue when you're ready to move on.
Welcome, guildmaster! I'm the Guild Trainer, and I'm here to guide you through setting up your own mercenary Guild. Let's start with you guiding the Brute through this first tutorial.
With only a single hit point remaining, any amount of damage getting through will cause the Brute to become exhausted. As such, you will have to avoid any incoming damage by burning cards.
Nice work on not dying, but the objective was to kill the Bandit Archer Elite. Perhaps there's an ability card you could use with an Attack that will do enough damage in one go? Have another try.
Burn a card to avoid the damage
What about Looting the chest? It may sound painful, but you'll have to step on that trap to reach it next time.
Mercenaries that leave gold behind don't tend to be particularly successful; remember to end the turn on gold when you have the opportunity!
Ouch! A Short Rest is great when you need some cards immediately, but lacks the healing component of a Long Rest... Be sure to select the right rest next time!
Whoops. The Brute had only one hit point remaining which means you couldn’t afford to take any more damage... Try again, but this time choose to burn one of your available cards to avoid the damage.
Select a remaining action and skip it to end your turn

Before you rush headlong into that trap, you might want to heal up a bit first. I’d suggest taking a Long Rest which will let you Heal 2 and recover enough discarded cards, allowing you to withstand the 2 damage from the trap and reach the chest.

Bosh! Overwhelming Assault is a powerful card, but such power comes at a cost. Actions like Overwhelming Assault's top half with the flaming card icon in the corner cause the card to be burned once used, meaning you can’t use it again in this scenario. Some enemies drop gold after being killed. Generally, to grab it, you need to finish your turn standing on hex with pile of gold. Try to do it with your remaining Action!
Move onto the chest and skip any remaining movement
Move onto the trap and take the damage
Kill the Archer in a single Attack
Good job! As you can see you can use only part of your action and skip the remaining parts if you don't want to use them!
Select a discarded card to burn

With no enemies left and your objectives complete, the scenario normally would have ended already, leaving that chest behind. However, for the sake of training, I'm going to set you another objective: Loot the chest!
You need to be able to move onto the gold to Loot it after you kill the Archer. Perhaps there's an ability card you could use with an Attack that will do enough damage in one hit? Have another go.
Ooh, those Boots of Striding will come in handy next time you need a little extra mobility. Right, onto the next part of your training!
Welcome to your first training mission! You've learned the basics, but now you must help the gravely wounded Brute deal with a Bandit Archer Elite, a stronger enemy with greater stats than normal!
Press Skip Movement to finish your turn
Use "Boots of Striding" to gain additional movement
